items.title: Guidelines for Post-Market Surveillance and Reporting of Medical Devices and In Vitro Diagnostics (IVDs)
items.source: National Agency for Food and Drug Administration and Control
items.why: Enhances regulatory oversight and risk management of medical devices and IVDs post-market, reducing risks to patient safety and improving public confidence in regulated products.
items.action: Implementation of feedback collection, data analysis, risk classification, and corrective action processes.

items.title: Innovative Product TRUPULSE™ Generator and Varipulse™ Bi-directional Catheter Approved for Marketing
items.why: This approval introduces a novel non-thermal pulsed electric field technology for atrial fibrillation treatment, which may impact clinical practice and regulatory post-marketing surveillance.
items.action: Implement enhanced post-marketing surveillance processes for these devices.

items.title: Understanding your post-market responsibilities for medical devices
items.why: Ensures continuous monitoring and management of medical device safety and performance to protect public health and patient safety.
items.action: Implement or update procedures for adverse event reporting, market actions, and conformity assessment notifications

items.title: Urgent Field Safety Notice for IntelliVue Patient Monitor by Philips Medizin Systeme Böblingen GmbH
items.why: This update is critical for patient safety and regulatory compliance in medical device monitoring and usage.
items.action: Implement safety measures as per manufacturer advisory.

items.title: Reino Unido. Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA). Alertas, retiro de productos e información de seguridad: medicamentos y dispositivos médicos
items.source: Dirección General de Medicamentos, Insumos y Drogas
items.why: Ensures timely communication of safety risks and regulatory actions to healthcare providers and manufacturers to mitigate patient harm and maintain compliance.
items.action: Implement recall and safety notice procedures as per MHRA guidance.

items.title: Regulatory Requirements of Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AI/ML) Enabled Medical Devices
items.source: South African Health Products Regulatory Authority
items.why: The update addresses the rapid advancement of AI/ML technologies in healthcare, emphasizing the need for clear regulatory frameworks to mitigate risks and ensure safe integration into the healthcare system.
items.action: Implementation of clinical validation, risk management, and post-market surveillance processes specific to AI/ML devices.

items.title: Innovative Product TRUPULSE™ Generator and Varipulse™ Bi-directional Catheter Approved for Marketing
items.why: This approval introduces new non-thermal pulsed electric field technology for atrial fibrillation treatment, potentially improving patient safety and treatment outcomes.
items.action: Implement and maintain post-marketing surveillance processes for the approved devices.

items.title: Direct healthcare professional communication on the risk of atrial fibrillation associated with the use of medicinal products containing Omega-3-acid ethyl esters
items.source: Hrvatska agencija za lijekove i medicinske proizvode
items.why: This update highlights a dose-dependent risk of atrial fibrillation in patients with cardiovascular disease or risk factors, impacting patient safety and treatment decisions.
items.action: Implement procedures for healthcare professional communication and adverse event reporting.

items.title: Fimean selvitys: Lääkinnällisinä laitteina säänneltyjä ohjelmistoja käytetään laajasti terveydenhuollossa
items.why: The widespread use of regulated software in healthcare underscores the importance of regulatory compliance to ensure patient safety and proper market functioning.
items.action: Enhance processes for defining product intended use and regulatory classification.

items.title: DE-1
items.source: Swissmedic, Swiss Agency for Therapeutic Products
items.why: The defect may prevent the guidewire from passing through the catheter hub, potentially delaying medical procedures, posing operational and patient safety risks.
items.action: Implement immediate product withdrawal and notification procedures; manage product returns

items.title: DE-1
items.source: Swissmedic, Swiss Agency for Therapeutic Products
items.why: The communication failure can lead to interruption in infusion delivery, potentially causing patient harm including serious injury or death, making immediate action critical.
items.action: Implement immediate communication and notification processes to inform all affected users and departments; manage acknowledgment and reporting workflows.

items.title: MU600_00_006e_MB Procurement of medical devices in health institutions (PDF, 486 kB, 20.03.2026)
items.source: Swissmedic, Swiss Agency for Therapeutic Products
items.why: Ensures healthcare institutions comply with updated Swiss medical device regulations aligned with EU MDR and IVDR, mitigating risks related to device safety, traceability, and regulatory compliance.
items.action: Implement processes to record and maintain UDI data for implantable class III devices; verify conformity markings on devices procured.
